$comment: ADOS reference archetypes for repository cataloging. Archetype means reusable kind of thing being delivered; not domain/tool stack/file extension.
drift_guards: Archetype must not mean Domain, file extension, or tool stack.
Choose primary archetype by accepted delivery, not by the most visible single file.
Secondary archetype requires distinct material contribution.

archetypes.label: User-facing application
archetypes.when_to_use: A runnable user-facing product is the accepted delivery and acceptance turns on its behavior/workflow.

archetypes.label: Runtime package / library
archetypes.when_to_use: A reusable runtime, library, module, package, or SDK consumed by other software.

archetypes.label: AI agent / prompt / skill
archetypes.when_to_use: The delivery is a prompt, system message, agent definition, skill, plugin, hook, or LLM behavior contract.

archetypes.label: Automation / diagnostic script
archetypes.when_to_use: A script/tool automates a task or diagnoses a system.

archetypes.label: Data extraction / processing pipeline
archetypes.when_to_use: The delivery transforms/extracts/parses/analyzes data into structured output.

archetypes.label: Research / analysis project
archetypes.when_to_use: A durable investigation producing findings, comparisons, or recommendations grounded in evidence.

archetypes.label: Controlled spec / schema / glossary
archetypes.when_to_use: The delivery is a governed specification: JSON Schema, ontology, glossary, standard, or contract.

archetypes.label: Repository catalog / software intelligence bank
archetypes.when_to_use: The delivery collects repository metadata and semantic knowledge into queryable banks.

archetypes.label: Semantic memory / recall platform
archetypes.when_to_use: The delivery stores/retrieves evidence-grounded semantic memory.

archetypes.label: Agent orchestration framework
archetypes.when_to_use: The delivery coordinates multiple agents, roles, workflows, or tool calls.

archetypes.label: MCP server / tool bridge
archetypes.when_to_use: The delivery exposes tools/resources through Model Context Protocol or similar tool bridge.
